title: fraxinus cuspidata var. macropetala
intro: Fraxinus cuspidata var. macropetala, also known as the large-petaled ash, is a variety of the ash tree (Fraxinus cuspidata) native to China. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Fraxinus cuspidata var. macropetala include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory infections. Fraxinus cuspidata var. macropetala can be prepared as a decoction, made by steeping the bark or leaves in hot water, or as a tincture, extracted with a solvent like ethanol.
